description: Verification overrides for AI App Builders competitive analysis

dreamflow::gh_native.note: Built-in Source Control panel. Connect via repo HTTPS URL + Personal Access Token (PAT). Once connected, Dreamflow tracks file changes and supports common Git operations inside the UI (branching, committing/pushing, pulling/merging, conflict resolution, disconnect).

evidence.title: Dreamflow Git Integration - Full Workflow Support
evidence.snippet: Documentation states: "Dreamflow includes a built-in Source Control panel that supports common Git operations including branching, committing/pushing, pulling/merging, and conflict resolution. Connect via repo HTTPS URL + Personal Access Token (PAT)." This confirms native Git integration with full workflow support directly in the UI.

lovable::gh_native.note: GitHub becomes the single source of truth once connected. Two-way sync is supported: edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and edits in GitHub appear back in Lovable. Connection depends on stable repo path (org/account/repo name).

evidence.title: Lovable GitHub Integration - Two-Way Sync
evidence.snippet: Documentation explicitly states: "Single source of truth: When connected, your code lives in GitHub, not separately in Lovable. Two-way sync: Edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and vice versa." Further details: "Sync automatically: Edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and changes in GitHub sync back on the default branch (main)." This confirms native GitHub integration with bidirectional synchronization.

vibecode::gh_native.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

builder::gh_native.note: Repo-connected workflow designed around pull requests. Changes are packaged and submitted as PRs from inside the product (Send PR), reviewed in a Pull requests tab, and further edits can be requested by commenting on the PR and tagging @builderio-bot.

evidence.title: Builder.io GitHub Integration - PR-Native Workflow
evidence.snippet: Documentation states: "Builder Projects connects directly to your repositories, letting you visually edit code and create applications with AI assistance." Further details: "To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar. In the Pull requests tab, review the proposed changes. To request additional edits, comment in the pull request and tag @builderio-bot with specific instructions." This confirms native GitHub integration with PR-centric workflow.

bolt::gh_native.note: GitHub integration with frequent auto-commits and regular fetch. Bolt creates a commit whenever a change 'doesn't break the project', and checks GitHub periodically (docs mention every 30 seconds) to pull in external updates.

evidence.title: Bolt.new GitHub Integration - Auto-Commit System
evidence.snippet: Documentation explicitly states: "Bolt integrates directly with GitHub, handling all commit and update operations automatically." Details on auto-commit: "Bolt saves your work automatically. Every time you make a change that doesn't break the project, Bolt creates a commit for you. It also checks GitHub every 30 seconds for any updates made outside Bolt and pulls those in." This confirms native GitHub integration with automatic synchronization.

dreamflow::import_repo.note: Supports importing existing Flutter repos via 'Clone Codebase'. Limitations include Flutter-only (non-Flutter/monorepo may fail), and projects that require local servers/background processes or private dependencies may fail. Codegen scripts can't be executed inside Dreamflow; generated files must be produced externally and committed.

evidence.title: Dreamflow's Flutter Repo Import Limitations
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Dreamflow currently supports Flutter repositories only. If your repository contains non-Flutter code or multiple projects (like a monorepo), the import may fail.' It also mentions, 'Projects requiring local API servers or background processes during development will fail to run inside Dreamflow.' Additionally, 'You cannot currently execute scripts inside of the Dreamflow builder. If the imported project depends on code generation, you will need to generate Dart files outside of Dreamflow and commit them to the repo.' These quotes confirm the limitations described in the claim.

lovable::import_repo.note: Lovable FAQ: importing existing repos is not supported.

evidence.title: Lovable does not support importing existing GitHub repos
evidence.snippet: "Can I import an existing GitHub repo into Lovable? No. You can only export from Lovable to GitHub, not the other way around." This quote explicitly states that importing existing GitHub repositories into Lovable is not supported, confirming the claimed status.

vibecode::import_repo.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

builder::import_repo.note: Connects to existing repositories as the basis for Projects. Intended for working against real codebases with PR-based delivery to the repo (not an export-only model).

evidence.title: Builder.io Connects to Git Repositories for Projects
evidence.snippet: The documentation states, 'Builder Projects connects directly to your repositories, letting you visually edit code and create applications with AI assistance.' It further explains, 'To connect an existing Git repository: On the Projects page, click Connect Repo. Connect to your Git provider, such as GitHub, Azure DevOps, or Bitbucket and grant access to all or selected repositories.' This supports the claim that Builder.io connects to existing repositories as the basis for Projects.

bolt::import_repo.note: Docs explicitly include an 'Import an existing repository' flow as part of the GitHub integration. Bolt then syncs via commits/fetch (rather than PR creation inside the tool).

evidence.title: Bolt's GitHub Integration Supports Importing Repositories
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To import a project into Bolt from one of your GitHub repositories, follow the steps below: ... Use the drop-down menu to select the repository you’d like to import, or Click the Import from URL button, then enter your GitHub repository URL.' This confirms that Bolt includes an 'Import an existing repository' flow as part of its GitHub integration.

dreamflow::create_repo.note: Dreamflow cannot create repositories. Documentation states you must create a new blank repository on your Git provider (GitHub, GitLab, etc.) before connecting it in Dreamflow. The repo creation happens entirely outside of Dreamflow.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Requires Pre-Existing Repository
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To connect a Dreamflow project to Git repo: Open your existing project in Dreamflow. Go to the Source Control Panel on the left sidebar and click Connect Repository. In the dialog, provide: Repository URL  Paste the HTTPS URL of your new blank repository. If you haven't already created one, you can create a new repository on your Git provider (such as GitHub) before proceeding.' This confirms that Dreamflow does not create repositories - you must create them manually on GitHub/GitLab first.

lovable::create_repo.note: Connect flow creates a repo in chosen org.

evidence.title: Lovable GitHub Integration Creates Repository
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'A new GitHub repository is created for your project, and sync begins automatically.' This confirms that when you connect a project to GitHub through Lovable, a repository is indeed created in the chosen organization.

vibecode::create_repo.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

builder::create_repo.note: Builder.io can only connect to existing repositories. Documentation shows "Connect Repo" workflow for existing repos only, with no mention of creating new repositories from within the product.

evidence.title: Repository Creation Feature Status
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To connect an existing Git repository: On the Projects page, click Connect Repo.' The workflow only supports connecting to existing repositories. No functionality for creating new repositories from within Builder.io is documented.

bolt::create_repo.note: GitHub integration supports creating a new repo from a Bolt project (documented as part of the integration's workflows).

evidence.title: GitHub Integration for Creating Repositories
evidence.snippet: "Create a new repository from a Bolt project" - This section of the documentation explicitly outlines the steps to create a new GitHub repository from a Bolt project, confirming the feature's existence. It states: 'To create a repository from your Bolt project... enter a name for your new repository. Click Create repository.' This confirms the claimed feature.

dreamflow::branching.note: Branching is supported directly in the Source Control panel: create a branch, switch branches, and refresh branch list. Dreamflow can also pull changes and merge commits from the remote branch.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Git Branching Feature Verification
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'You can easily manage branches directly within the platform, i.e., create new ones, switch between existing ones, and refresh your branch list when needed.' Additionally, it mentions: 'Dreamflow will fetch and merge any new commits from your remote branch.' These quotes confirm that branching, switching branches, refreshing branch lists, and merging commits from a remote branch are supported.

evidence.title: GitHub Branch Switching as Experimental Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Enable experimental features like GitHub branch switching to test new capabilities before they become generally available.' This indicates that GitHub branch switching is indeed a Labs/experimental feature, supporting the claim that it is in a LIMITED status.

vibecode::branching.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

builder::branching.note: Branch/PR workflow is supported. PR creation is the documented delivery mechanism ('Send PR'), and further edits can be requested via PR comments tagging @builderio-bot (adds commits to the same PR).

evidence.title: Builder.io Supports Branch/PR Workflow
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar.' It further explains, 'To request additional edits, comment in the pull request and tag @builderio-bot with specific instructions. The bot applies the changes to the same PR.' These quotes confirm the support for a branch/PR workflow and the use of PR comments to request further edits.

bolt::branching.note: Branch switching is supported as part of GitHub integration. Merge is not described as an in-app operation (workflow expects merging in GitHub).

evidence.title: Branch Switching Supported, Merging Not In-App
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'You can create new branches in Bolt or on GitHub.' and 'Bolt currently doesn’t support merging branches in-app. You need to merge branches in GitHub.' These quotes confirm that branch switching is supported as part of GitHub integration, but merging is not an in-app operation.

dreamflow::pr_creation.note: Supports committing and pushing changes directly through Source Control panel.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Push Changes Documentation
evidence.snippet: To push changes from your project to the remote repository: Make edits to your app. Check Source Control > Changes. The modified file will be tagged M and listed there. (Optional) Click View Diff to compare your edit with the previous version. Use AI to automatically generate a Commit Message, or enter your own message to describe what has changed. Click the Push icon to commit and push the update to your connected repository.

lovable::pr_creation.note: Auto-commits and syncs changes to GitHub as the single source of truth.

evidence.title: Lovable GitHub Integration - Pull Requests and Collaboration
evidence.snippet: Collaborate easily: Invite developers, use pull requests, branches, and code reviews.

vibecode::pr_creation.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

builder::pr_creation.note: PR creation is explicitly supported: make updates, click 'Send PR', review in Pull requests tab. Additional edits can be requested by commenting on the PR and tagging @builderio-bot; the bot applies changes to the same PR as new commits.

evidence.title: Builder.io PR Creation and Editing Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar. In the Pull requests tab, review the proposed changes. To request additional edits, comment in the pull request and tag @builderio-bot with specific instructions. The bot applies the changes to the same PR.' This confirms that Builder.io supports PR creation and allows additional edits via @builderio-bot.

bolt::pr_creation.note: Pull requests are not supported within Bolt.new and must be created in GitHub.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Pull Request Support
evidence.snippet: Bolt.new does not support creating pull requests within the product. While it integrates with GitHub for committing and pushing code, pull requests must be created and managed directly in GitHub.

dreamflow::merge_in_product.note: Supports merging via pull operation - fetches and merges commits from remote branch.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Pull Changes and Merge Support
evidence.snippet: To update your project with the latest code from the remote repository, click the pull icon in the Source Control panel. Dreamflow will fetch and merge any new commits from your remote branch.

lovable::merge_in_product.note: Supports branch switching (Labs feature) allowing work on feature branches and merging into main.

evidence.title: Lovable GitHub Branch Switching and Merging
evidence.snippet: Lovable supports branch switching and management, allowing for a professional development workflow. Enable Branch Switching in Settings → Labs. Once enabled, you can switch between different GitHub branches directly within the Lovable project interface. You can create a new branch (e.g., dev) in GitHub, switch to it in Lovable, make updates, and test changes safely. Once testing is complete, you can merge your feature branch into the main branch, which will update your live project.

vibecode::merge_in_product.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

evidence.title: Absence of GitLab Workflow Merge Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Follow these steps to work with your connected repository: Make changes to the repository with Projects. Click Send PR to create a pull request. Review and merge the pull request in your GitLab instance.' This indicates that while Builder.io allows creating pull requests, the actual merging of workflows must be conducted within the GitLab instance, not directly through Builder.io.

bolt::merge_in_product.note: Not supported in-app. Must merge branches in GitHub.

evidence.title: Bolt Lacks In-App Merging Feature
evidence.snippet: "Bolt currently doesn’t support merging branches in-app. You need to merge branches in GitHub." This statement directly confirms that Bolt does not have an in-tool workflow for merging branches, supporting the claim that merging is positioned to be done in GitHub instead.

dreamflow::multi_user_collab.note: Docs explicitly warn multi-user collaboration within the same repository is not supported. If multiple Dreamflow projects attempt to use the same repo, the system detects and restricts it; team collaboration is positioned as an Enterprise plan discussion.

evidence.title: Multi-user Collaboration Limitation in Dreamflow
evidence.snippet: "warning Currently, Dreamflow does not support multi-user collaboration within the same repository. If multiple Dreamflow projects attempt to use the same repo, the system will detect and restrict it. For team collaboration, please reach out regarding the Enterprise Plan." This quote confirms that Dreamflow explicitly warns against multi-user collaboration within the same repository and suggests that team collaboration is a feature discussed under the Enterprise plan.

lovable::multi_user_collab.note: Collaboration is framed around GitHub workflows because GitHub is the source of truth with two-way sync. The doc does not describe real-time multi-user editing inside Lovable itself; team flow is primarily via repo, branches, reviews, and stable repo path.

evidence.title: GitHub-Centric Collaboration in Lovable
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Collaborate easily: Invite developers, use pull requests, branches, and code reviews.' It also mentions: 'Edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and changes in GitHub sync back on the default branch (main).' These quotes confirm that collaboration is structured around GitHub workflows, supporting the claim that real-time multi-user editing within Lovable itself is not described.

vibecode::multi_user_collab.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Does Not Support GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Reviewed Vibecode documentation at https://www.vibecodeapp.com/docs - no mention of GitHub integration, repository importing, or Git-based workflows. Documentation focuses on SSH export feature only. Absence of any GitHub functionality in official docs confirms these features are not supported.

evidence.title: PR-Centric Workflow for Teams in Builder.io
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar.' This indicates that Builder.io supports a PR-centric workflow by allowing users to create pull requests directly from the Visual Editor.

bolt::multi_user_collab.note: Docs state Bolt doesn't support GitHub organization accounts (individual accounts only). Team collaboration therefore relies on GitHub-side workflows rather than org-wide integration inside Bolt.

evidence.title: Bolt GitHub Integration Limitations
evidence.snippet: The documentation states, 'Bolt currently doesn’t support GitHub organization accounts. Sign up for one if you don’t already have one.' This confirms that Bolt only supports individual GitHub accounts, not organization accounts, which aligns with the claim that team collaboration relies on GitHub-side workflows rather than org-wide integration inside Bolt.

evidence.title: Agent Panel in Dreamflow Workspace
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Agent Panel: AI-powered development assistant that helps you build and modify your Flutter app through natural language conversations. Features streaming conversations, context management, screenshot integration, and support for multiple AI models. The agent panel is to the right of the workspace.' This confirms that the agent operates over the project workspace.

evidence.title: Documentation of Debugging Guidance in Lovable
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Fix build errors, debug unexpected behavior, resolve preview issues, and recover from common problems step by step.' It also provides a step-by-step guide: 'Click the Try to Fix button when an error shows up. Lovable will scan your logs, detect the issue, and attempt a quick fix.' This indicates that Lovable offers debugging guidance.

vibecode::codebase_aware_prompting.note: Prompts apply to generated project; scope not clearly defined in docs.

evidence.title: Vibecode Uses Claude Code for AI Generation
evidence.snippet: No Coding Required - Build apps using natural language prompts. Powered by Claude Code - Uses the most advanced AI coding agent available. While the documentation confirms AI generation from prompts using Claude Code (which is inherently codebase-aware), the specific scope and capabilities of codebase analysis are not explicitly detailed.

builder::codebase_aware_prompting.note: Supports refining applications through prompts in Chat tab with immediate code changes.

evidence.title: Builder.io Refine Application by Prompt
evidence.snippet: To refine your application: Go to the prompt field embedded on the left, within the Chat tab. Write a new prompt specifying what you'd like changed. Click Enter. Builder immediately begins to apply the changes. This demonstrates that Builder.io can analyze and modify existing application code based on natural language prompts.

bolt::codebase_aware_prompting.note: Prompts operate over project/files in Bolt environment.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Prompting Effectively
evidence.snippet: Documentation covers best practices for prompting in Bolt, including how to make changes to your project through prompts. This demonstrates that Bolt supports codebase-aware prompting where the AI can reason over and modify project files based on natural language instructions.

dreamflow::repo_as_ai_input.note: Only works with primary connected repo, not additional repos for context.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Single Repo Limitation
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow supports connecting a project to a GitHub repository for context, but does not support simultaneously connecting multiple external repos for additional context. Multi-user collaboration within the same repository is not supported, and connecting to multiple external repositories for context is not supported in standard workflows.

lovable::repo_as_ai_input.note: Docs: cannot import existing repo into Lovable.

evidence.title: Importing Existing GitHub Repositories Not Supported
evidence.snippet: "Can I import an existing GitHub repo into Lovable? No. You can only export from Lovable to GitHub, not the other way around." This quote directly confirms that Lovable does not support importing existing GitHub repositories, thus supporting the claimed status and note.

vibecode::repo_as_ai_input.note: Not documented. Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration or any Git-based workflows.

evidence.title: Vibecode Lacks GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Vibecode documentation makes no mention of GitHub integration, repository connections, or Git-based workflows. The platform does not support connecting to existing repositories or using repos as AI input context.

builder::repo_as_ai_input.note: Connect existing repos; additional repos for context.

evidence.title: Builder.io Supports Connecting and Adding Repositories
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To connect an existing Git repository: On the Projects page, click Connect Repo. Connect to your Git provider, such as GitHub, Azure DevOps, or Bitbucket and grant access to all or selected repositories.' Additionally, it mentions: 'To add repositories: Click the three dots on the project tile and choose Project settings. Expand Workspace Settings. Click the Add Repository button.' This confirms that Builder.io allows connecting existing repositories and adding additional ones for context.

bolt::repo_as_ai_input.note: Can import repo as project, but not additional repos for context.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Repository Import Limitation
evidence.snippet: To import a project into Bolt from one of your GitHub repositories: Use the drop-down menu to select the repository you'd like to import, or Click the Import from URL button, then enter your GitHub repository URL. Click Choose this repository. Bolt will load your repository as a new Bolt project. This shows Bolt imports a repo as the primary project, not as additional context alongside other repos.

dreamflow::file_component_targeting.note: Supports Inspect Mode (click widget to jump to code) and component-specific editing.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Component-Specific Editing and Inspect Mode
evidence.snippet: DreamFlow allows targeted modification of specific files, components, or UI areas. Features include: Inspect Mode (click on a widget in live preview to jump directly to that code), component-specific editing (use AI agent to add properties to specific Flutter widgets), focused conversation threads for iterative changes to specific codebase parts, and visual/code synergy enabling AI to modify specific UI areas. Users can mention specific files in prompts to ensure correct components are modified.

lovable::file_component_targeting.note: Achievable through prompting strategies (specify files to modify/avoid), but no built-in UI feature.

evidence.title: Lovable File Targeting Through Prompting
evidence.snippet: To ensure the AI only updates relevant files, provide clear instructions. You can guide the AI with prompts like: 'Please refrain from altering pages X or Y and focus changes solely on page Z.' While Lovable lacks a built-in file locking system or UI-based component selection, you can achieve file targeting through careful prompting strategies that specify which files to modify or avoid.

vibecode::file_component_targeting.note: Code Editor available on Pro, Max and Ultra tiers allows viewing and editing project source code.

evidence.title: Vibecode Code Editor (Pro Tier)
evidence.snippet: Code Editor Requires Pro. View and edit your project source code. Available on Pro, Max and Ultra.

builder::file_component_targeting.note: Codebase search allows referencing any file in the repository. Type @ in the prompt to refer a file.

evidence.title: Builder.io Codebase Search
evidence.snippet: Codebase search: Any file in the repository. Reference existing files in the connected repository. Type @ in the prompt to refer a file.

bolt::file_component_targeting.note: In Code view, right-click files and select 'Target file' to limit Bolt to specific files.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Target File Feature
evidence.snippet: Limit Bolt to specific files: Open a Bolt project. Click the code icon (<>) in the top center of your screen to switch to Code view. Right-click the files you want to focus on. Click Target file.

dreamflow::diff_aware_prompting.note: Agentic edits with diffs. Supports surgical updates across AI Prompt, Visual Canvas, and Source Code with widget-aware context for targeted edits.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Agentic Edits with Diffs
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow supports diff-aware prompting through its agentic edits feature, allowing AI to perform surgical updates across the AI Prompt, Visual Canvas, and Source Code within its tri-surface workspace. The platform enables checkpoint rollbacks similar to Git reverts and uses widget-aware context for targeted AI edits. According to Dreamflow's launch details on Hacker News, the tool specifically includes agentic edits with diffs.

lovable::diff_aware_prompting.note: Computes diffs to update only precisely modified lines. Uses AST-based generation with Hot Module Replacement for instant updates without full-file regeneration.

evidence.title: Lovable Diff-Aware Visual Edits
evidence.snippet: Thanks to Vite and our persistent Dev Servers, we can immediately trigger a Hot Module Replacement (HMR), refreshing the preview without requiring an explicit page reload. Upon saving changes, a chain of events executes instantly: Clean, standard-compliant JSX/TSX is generated from the modified AST. Diffs are computed to update only precisely modified lines.

vibecode::diff_aware_prompting.note: Pinch to Build uses incremental patching to produce additive changes/previewable code patches. Provides AI with context only for isolated components, avoiding full app regeneration.

evidence.title: Vibecode Pinch to Build Incremental Patching
evidence.snippet: Vibecode utilizes Pinch to Build as a gesture-based method to isolate specific on-screen elements, providing the AI with context only for that component rather than the entire codebase. This system employs incremental patching to produce additive changes or previewable code patches that are injected into the existing structure, avoiding a full app regeneration on every change.

builder::diff_aware_prompting.note: Keeps a tree representation of the component to create fast surgical updates instead of regenerating entire code over and over.

evidence.title: Builder.io Tree Representation for Surgical Updates
evidence.snippet: Speed of iteration is important. For a tool used daily, how fast the AI can generate code in response to changes matters, as the additional time AI spends thinking starts to add up. Many AI tools simply regenerate the entire code over and overnewer, smart AI codegen tools instead keep a tree representation of the component to create fast surgical updates.

bolt::diff_aware_prompting.note: Diffs feature stops Bolt from rewriting entire files during small changes. Can save millions of tokens by avoiding full-file regeneration.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Diffs Feature for Token Savings
evidence.snippet: The diffs feature helps save tokens but stays turned off by default. This feature stops Bolt from rewriting entire files during small changes and can save millions of tokens. Simple UI changes can eat up your monthly tokens faster without this setting.

dreamflow::persistent_prompt_memory.note: Agent Instructions provide high-level architectural patterns and library preferences that persist through the development cycle. Widget Tree maintains inherent UI structure memory.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Agent Instructions and Widget Tree Persistence
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow is built specifically for Flutter and focuses on a lead architect workflow. Agent Instructions: Persistent memory is handled via the Agent section, where you can provide high-level architectural patterns or specific libraries (e.g., always use Stripe for payments) that persist through the development cycle. Widget Tree Persistence: Because Dreamflow maintains a visual Widget Tree, the memory of your UI structure is inherently persistent; changes are applied as modifications to this tree rather than random code regenerations.

lovable::persistent_prompt_memory.note: Custom Knowledge acts as a project blueprint storing design rules, functionality requirements, and technical goals. Visual Scope Selection and Dev Mode maintain state across the project.

evidence.title: Lovable Custom Knowledge and Visual Scope Selection
evidence.snippet: Lovable uses a combination of built-in settings and visual scaffolding to manage project-wide context. Custom Knowledge: You can add Custom Knowledge to act as a blueprint for your project. This persistent memory stores specific design rules, functionality requirements, and technical goals that the AI refers to for every future edit. Visual Scope Selection: To support diff-aware edits, Lovable allows you to visually select specific elements on the screen. This tells the AI exactly where to focus, preventing it from touching other parts of the codebase. Dev Mode: For more direct control, Dev Mode allows you to edit files directly within the platform, maintaining state across the project.

vibecode::persistent_prompt_memory.note: Memory Bank files (.cursorrules, PROJECT_MEMORY.md) externalize long-term storage for tech stack shifts and API keys. Encourages tiny task approach to prevent context loss.

evidence.title: Vibecode Memory Bank Files
evidence.snippet: Vibe coding as a methodology (and specifically in apps like Vibe) often relies on externalizing memory to prevent context anxiety. Memory Bank Files: Many users implement a Memory Bank structure using files like .cursorrules or PROJECT_MEMORY.md. These files serve as long-term storage for tech stack shifts and API keys so the AI doesn't regress during iterative edits. Rules for Production: The platform encourages a tiny task approachsplitting projects into small, manageable prompts to ensure the AI doesn't lose track of project-level constraints.

builder::persistent_prompt_memory.note: .builderrules files for CLI, Visual Editor Instructions panel, and System Prompts for long-term constraints guide AI consistently across sessions.

evidence.title: Builder.io Persistent Memory Management
evidence.snippet: Builder.io manages persistent memory through both its visual interface and local configuration files. .builderrules Files: For developers using the CLI, you can create .builderrules files in your project root or subdirectories. The AI automatically scans these files to guide code generation consistently across sessions. Visual Editor Instructions: Within the web interface, you can add persistent context via the Instructions panel in the Generate tab. These instructions guide the AI on your content models, API usage, and specific layout patterns. Durable Policies: Builder also supports System Prompts for setting long-term constraints like Always use Stripe for payments, ensuring the AI doesn't forget core technical requirements.

bolt::persistent_prompt_memory.note: Project Knowledge provides persistent background instructions for goals, style expectations, terminology, constraints, and workflow habits. Also supports Personal Settings that apply across all projects.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Project Knowledge
evidence.snippet: Project Knowledge gives you a steady layer of background instructions that Bolt follows whenever you're working inside a specific project. It gives Bolt a reliable sense of context, allowing you to include things like goals, style expectations, terminology, constraints, and workflow habits. Instead of repeating these details in every prompt, you can place them in Project Knowledge so the model can use them automatically. You can also set Knowledge that applies to all of your projects in your Personal Settings. Anything added there takes priority over Project Knowledge.

dreamflow::screenshot_prompt_input.note: Add screenshots for design inspiration. Dreamflow Agent generates entire app structure, UI components, and functionality from text and image descriptions.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Screenshots for Design Inspiration
evidence.snippet: Start With a Prompt: Dreamflow's AI-powered app generation allows you to create fully functional, production-ready applications from simple text and image descriptions. Simply describe what you want to build, additionally add screenshots for design inspiration, and Dreamflow Agent will generate the entire app structure, UI components, and functionality for you.

lovable::screenshot_prompt_input.note: Paste or drag-and-drop screenshots from Figma designs or hand-drawn sketches. Lovable converts visual designs into functional code.

evidence.title: Lovable Figma and Sketch Screenshot Support
evidence.snippet: Using Figma: If you have a design in Figma, take a screenshot of any part of it. You can paste the screenshot directly into Lovable or drag-and-drop the image file. Once you press Enter, Lovable will convert your design into functional code. Using a sketch: Take a screenshot of your drawing, then paste or drag-and-drop it into Lovable. The platform will transform your sketch into working code.

vibecode::screenshot_prompt_input.note: Upload screenshots in AI chat to define design styles, create prototypes, and generate layouts. Screenshots alongside prompts create custom mixed designs for functional mobile apps.

evidence.title: Vibecode Screenshot Upload for Design
evidence.snippet: Vibecode allows users to upload screenshots into its AI chat interface to define design styles for projects or create prototypes that can then be refined using targeted reflections. Users can also upload screenshots alongside prompts to generate specific layouts and design elements without manual coding, enabling custom "mixed" designs that can be launched as functional mobile apps.

builder::screenshot_prompt_input.note: Include screenshots and images with prompts. AI uses them to understand issues or provide design inspiration for new features. Supports screenshots and PDF files.

evidence.title: Builder.io Screenshot and Image Support
evidence.snippet: Provide screenshots: AI understands your prompts better when you include images. Use screenshots to show issues that need fixing or provide design inspiration for new features. In the video below, both a screenshot and PDF file are used as inspiration for creating a new feature.

bolt::screenshot_prompt_input.note: Upload images to inform Bolt. Add images as examples of look and feel. Supports .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .gif, .webp, .svg. Drag-and-drop or attach via chat.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Image Upload Support
evidence.snippet: Add files to inform Bolt: You can give Bolt extra information by uploading files. To upload a file, either: In the bottom-left corner of the chatbox, click the plus icon, then click Attach a file. Drag and drop the file into the chatbox. For example, you can: Add an image as an example of the look and feel you want. Supported file types - Images: .jpg .jpeg .png .gif .webp .svg

dreamflow::figma_import.note: No direct Figma import. Use screenshots of Figma frames as visual context, or describe design system in prompts. Sister platform FlutterFlow has this feature, not Dreamflow.

evidence.title: Dreamflow No Direct Figma Import
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow does not currently support a direct "Design to Code" import feature for Figma in the same way its sister platform, FlutterFlow, does. Instead, Dreamflow focuses on an AI-first workflow where you generate UI through natural language prompts or by uploading screenshots for visual inspiration. While there is no "Connect to Figma" button yet, you can still leverage your Figma designs: Screenshot Reference: You can upload screenshots of your Figma frames to the Dreamflow Agent, which can then use them as visual context to scaffold screens and components. Prompt Engineering: Describe your Figma design system (colors, typography, and layouts) in the prompt to have the AI generate matching code.

lovable::figma_import.note: Integrates via Builder.io plugin partnership. Run Builder.io plugin in Figma, select frames, choose 'Export to Lovable'. Generates full-stack apps including Supabase backends.

evidence.title: Lovable Figma Integration via Builder.io
evidence.snippet: Lovable: Integrates with Figma primarily through a partnership with Builder.io. Method: You run the Builder.io plugin within Figma, select your frames, and choose the "Export to Lovable" option. Precision Details: This workflow allows Lovable to generate full-stack applications (including Supabase backends) directly from the imported UI. It also supports dragging and dropping screenshots of Figma designs as a visual prompt.

vibecode::figma_import.note: Mobile-first builder using natural language prompts and mobile interaction. No native Figma import feature. Supports source code downloads and SSH to editors.

evidence.title: Vibecode No Native Figma Import
evidence.snippet: Vibecode is a mobile-first AI app builder that primarily uses natural language prompts and mobile interaction, rather than direct Figma imports, for app generation. While it supports source code downloads and SSH connections to editors, it does not currently offer a native "Import from Figma" feature.

builder::figma_import.note: Most mature integration. Dedicated Figma plugin with Visual Copilot. One-click conversion to React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, HTML. CLI maps Figma layers to existing codebase components.

evidence.title: Builder.io Visual Copilot Figma Plugin
evidence.snippet: Builder.io: Provides the most mature integration through its Visual Copilot toolchain. Method: Uses a dedicated Figma plugin to export designs. Precision Details: Supports one-click conversion into React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, and HTML. It includes a Visual Copilot CLI that can map Figma layers directly to your existing codebase components to ensure design system consistency.

bolt::figma_import.note: Direct Figma Frame URL imports (no plugin). Paste 'Copy link to selection' URL into chatbox. Supports mid-project imports. Converts to React/Tailwind code.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Direct Figma URL Import
evidence.snippet: Bolt.new: Supports direct Figma Frame URL imports without needing a plugin. Method: Users paste a "Copy link to selection" URL from Figma directly into the Bolt.new chatbox or homepage. Precision Details: As of January 2026, Bolt supports mid-project imports, allowing you to add new Figma frames to an existing codebase without starting over. It converts designs into React/Tailwind code.

dreamflow::runtime_preview_as_input.note: Realtime Preview with AI agent assistance. Add debug outputs and monitor state changes as they happen. Keeps agent, visual canvas, and code in sync during live run session.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Realtime Preview State Monitoring
evidence.snippet: The platform features a Realtime Preview where the AI agent can help add debug outputs and monitor state changes as they happen. It focuses on keeping the agent, visual canvas, and code in sync during the live run session.

lovable::runtime_preview_as_input.note: Vision Language Model (VLM) agents take screenshots of website to determine next action. Users add screenshots to prompts for describing bugs or UX issues the AI needs to fix.

evidence.title: Lovable VLM-based Browser Testing
evidence.snippet: Lovable uses Vision Language Model (VLM) agents that take screenshots of the website to determine the next action. Users are encouraged to add screenshots to prompts specifically for describing bugs or UX issues the AI needs to fix.

vibecode::runtime_preview_as_input.note: Provides live preview (standard for AI builders), but no public documentation confirming runtime state or preview screenshots fed back to AI as structured input for automated iteration.

evidence.title: Vibecode Live Preview Only
evidence.snippet: While it provides a live preview (standard for AI builders), there is currently no public documentation or evidence confirming it supports feeding the active runtime state or preview screenshots back into the AI as a structured input for automated iteration.

builder::runtime_preview_as_input.note: Visual Copilot takes screenshots on demand for visual context. AI compares screenshots of different UI states to verify if code changes reflect intended visual outcome.

evidence.title: Builder.io Visual Copilot Screenshot-on-Demand
evidence.snippet: Builder's Visual Copilot can "take screenshots on demand" to give the AI agent visual context. It allows the AI to compare screenshots of different UI states to verify if code changes reflect the intended visual outcome.

bolt::runtime_preview_as_input.note: Built on StackBlitz WebContainers. AI automatically detects and fixes runtime errors from browser console. Discuss feature debugs projects using current project state as context.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Integrated Runtime Feedback
evidence.snippet: Built on StackBlitz WebContainers, Bolt allows the AI to automatically detect and fix runtime errors generated in the browser console. It also includes a discuss feature to debug projects by using the current project state as context for planning.

dreamflow::mcp_connectors.note: Agent-based architecture designed for tool-calling and real-time state monitoring, similar to MCP functionality, though not marketed using the MCP term.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Agent-based Tool-Calling Architecture
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow's agent-based architecture is designed for tool-calling and real-time state monitoring, similar to MCP functionality, though not marketed using the term.

lovable::mcp_connectors.note: Personal Connectors (MCP servers) for access to private tools during app creation. MCP servers documented (including custom servers + auth options).

evidence.title: Lovable Personal Connectors (MCP)
evidence.snippet: Lovable supports connecting Personal Connectors (MCP servers) for access to private tools during app creation.

vibecode::mcp_connectors.note: No official built-in MCP client documented. Users can export projects to Cursor, which natively supports MCP.

evidence.title: Vibecode Export to Cursor for MCP
evidence.snippet: Vibecodeapp.com does not have official documentation of a built-in MCP client, but users can export projects to Cursor, which natively supports MCP.

builder::mcp_connectors.note: Native support for connecting custom MCP servers to query application-specific databases. Fusion Integrations for developers.

evidence.title: Builder.io Fusion Integrations MCP Support
evidence.snippet: Builder.io offers native support for connecting custom MCP servers to query application-specific databases.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature in Bolt.new
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The sections cover various functionalities like managing projects, duplicating projects, and integrating with other tools, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

dreamflow::telemetry_as_context.note: Built-in data analytics dashboards in framework. Design philosophy encourages refining apps post-launch based on actual user behavior, but iterative feedback loop not fully automated.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Iterative Feedback Loop
evidence.snippet: It features built-in data analytics directly in the framework. The platform's design philosophy encourages refining apps post-launch based on actual user behavior tracked via these dashboards.

lovable::telemetry_as_context.note: Provides usage analytics for SaaS dashboards and generates live analytics for apps. AI input focuses on natural language feedback or external tools (n8n) rather than automated telemetry-to-code loop.

evidence.title: Lovable Analytical App Generation
evidence.snippet: While Lovable provides usage analytics for SaaS dashboards and generates live analytics dashboards for your app, the current AI input primarily focuses on natural language feedback or external tools via n8n rather than an automated telemetry-to-code feedback loop.

vibecode::telemetry_as_context.note: No public documentation indicating telemetry or analytics used as direct input for AI generation engine.

evidence.title: Vibecode Limited Telemetry Information
evidence.snippet: There is currently no public documentation indicating that Vibecode uses application telemetry or analytics as a direct input for its AI generation engine.

builder::telemetry_as_context.note: Smart Targeting uses historical API logs and telemetry to suggest targeting attribute values. Visual Editor AI uses data context to generate optimized layouts. Pass content interaction data to external providers.

evidence.title: Builder.io Smart Targeting Direct Integration
evidence.snippet: Its Smart Targeting feature uses historical API logs and telemetry to suggest targeting attribute values. Additionally, it allows you to pass content interaction data to external providers, while the Visual Editor AI can use this data context to generate optimized layouts.

bolt::telemetry_as_context.note: Built-in analytics on paid plans track traffic, top pages, 404 errors. AI helps embed third-party tracking code but doesn't automatically ingest metrics to propose code changes.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Passive Reporting
evidence.snippet: Projects on paid plans include built-in analytics to track traffic, top pages, and 404 errors. While the AI can help you embed third-party tracking code, it does not yet automatically ingest those performance metrics to propose code changes.

dreamflow::opinionated_backend.note: Deep native Supabase/Firebase support. Auto-generates client setup, schema updates, and OAuth login pages (OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant flow).

evidence.title: Dreamflow Deep Native Backend Support
evidence.snippet: It features built-in modules for both Supabase and Firebase. It automatically generates client setup code and handles schema updates for Supabase or Firestore without manual configuration files.

lovable::opinionated_backend.note: Supabase-native (powers Lovable Cloud). AI auto-generates database tables, Auth flows (one-click Google/GitHub OAuth), and Edge Functions via chat. Auto-handles callback URLs and token management.

evidence.title: Lovable Supabase-Native Integration
evidence.snippet: Lovable is heavily opinionated toward Supabase, which powers its "Lovable Cloud" infrastructure. The AI agent can automatically generate database tables, set up Auth flows, and even deploy Edge Functions via a single chat interface.

vibecode::opinionated_backend.note: No native one-click backend. Limited auth support (Vibecode Cloud beta). Users export to Cursor to manually integrate Supabase for auth and database.

evidence.title: Vibecode External Integration
evidence.snippet: Vibecode focuses on rapid UI "vibe coding." While it does not have a native, one-click backend dashboard, users typically export their code to Cursor to manually integrate Supabase for authentication and database connectivity.

builder::opinionated_backend.note: Integration-agnostic. Supabase MCP server + Firebase Studio partnership. Enterprise SSO (OIDC, SAML 2.0). Bridges backend data and authenticated sessions into visual editor.

evidence.title: Builder.io Integration-Agnostic Backend
evidence.snippet: While Builder is primarily a visual CMS, it provides a dedicated Supabase MCP server and has a strategic partnership with Firebase Studio to sync designs and code. It uses these connectors to bridge real backend data into its visual editor.

bolt::opinionated_backend.note: Provider-agnostic with Bolt Database for new projects. Dedicated Auth module (Google SSO, GitHub login). Auto-configures Supabase Auth for OAuth flows and user sessions. bolt.diy has structured Supabase paths.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Provider-Agnostic Approach
evidence.snippet: Bolt does not have a single "opinionated" backend choice; instead, it allows you to prompt the AI to set up any provider. However, the community-driven bolt.diy version often includes more structured setup paths for providers like Supabase. New projects use Bolt Database.

dreamflow::db_state_as_context.note: Architecture Awareness. Uses architecture.md file as primary AI context to track intended DB state. AI ensures code generation respects established data structures in integrated Supabase/Firebase environments.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Architecture Awareness
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow uses an architecture.md file as a primary AI context source, which tracks the intended DB state. The AI uses this persistent context to ensure code generation respects established data structures within its integrated Supabase/Firebase environments.

lovable::db_state_as_context.note: Schema-Aware Generation. Tight Supabase integration maintains constant awareness of existing PostgreSQL tables and types. AI inspects tables to ensure new features correctly reference column names and foreign key relationships.

evidence.title: Lovable Schema-Aware Generation
evidence.snippet: Lovable's tight Supabase Integration means the AI maintains a constant awareness of your existing PostgreSQL tables and types. It can inspect existing tables to ensure new features correctly reference existing column names and foreign key relationships.

vibecode::db_state_as_context.note: Manual Mapping. Focuses on visual/frontend vibe. No evidence AI can autonomously crawl or inspect existing production database to inform code output.

evidence.title: Vibecode Manual Mapping
evidence.snippet: Currently, Vibecode focuses on the visual and frontend "vibe." There is no evidence in their official documentation that their AI can autonomously crawl or inspect an existing production database to inform its code output.

builder::db_state_as_context.note: Native Connectivity. Data Connectors and Supabase MCP Server allow AI to directly inspect live data and schemas. Visual Copilot maps UI components to actual database fields in real-time.

evidence.title: Builder.io Native Connectivity
evidence.snippet: Through its Data Connectors and the Supabase MCP Server, Builder's AI can directly inspect live data and schemas. This allows the Visual Copilot to map UI components to actual database fields in real-time.

bolt::db_state_as_context.note: Session-Based Context. Standard web version has limited visibility into external databases unless schema explicitly shared in chat. bolt.diy fork supports MCP server configs to query local/remote database metadata.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Session-Based Context
evidence.snippet: In its standard web version, the AI has limited visibility into external live databases unless the schema is explicitly shared in the chat. However, the bolt.diy fork supports MCP server configurations that can allow an AI agent to query local or remote database metadata to inform generation.

dreamflow::secrets_handling_documented.note: Variable-Based Context. Manages secrets via Environment Variable configuration. AI references variables (Stripe, SendGrid) using .env syntax. AI knows secret exists without plain-text in prompts.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Variable-Based Context
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow manages secrets via its Environment Variable configuration. It allows the AI to reference variables for Stripe or SendGrid by using standard .env syntax, ensuring the AI "knows" a secret exists for its logic without the user having to paste the plain-text key into the prompt.

lovable::secrets_handling_documented.note: Integrated Secret Management. Dedicated Secrets and Environment Variables section in project settings. Manages Supabase service role keys and API keys (Stripe) securely. AI references by name, actual values hidden from frontend.

evidence.title: Lovable Integrated Secret Management
evidence.snippet: Lovable provides a dedicated Secrets and Environment Variables section in the project settings. It specifically manages Supabase service role keys and API keys (like Stripe) securely, ensuring the AI can reference them by name in the code while keeping the actual values hidden from the frontend.

vibecode::secrets_handling_documented.note: Manual/Export focus. Limited native infrastructure for secret hosting. Users scaffold UI/logic, then export to local environment or Cursor to manually populate .env with passwords and keys.

evidence.title: Vibecode Manual/Export Focus
evidence.snippet: Vibecode currently has limited native infrastructure for secret hosting. Most users utilize the platform for UI and logic scaffolding and then export to a local environment or Cursor to manually populate a local .env file with database passwords and encryption keys.

builder::secrets_handling_documented.note: Secure Integration Vault. Private Keys and Secrets manager for Fusion integrations. Securely stores OAuth Client Secrets and Service Account credentials. Used on backend for authenticated API requests, kept away from client-side.

evidence.title: Builder.io Secure Integration Vault
evidence.snippet: Builder uses a Private Keys and Secrets manager for its "Fusion" integrations. This allows you to securely store OAuth Client Secrets and Service Account credentials. These are used on the Builder.io backend when making authenticated API requests, keeping them away from the client-side browser.

bolt::secrets_handling_documented.note: Client-Side Secrets. Uses .env file in WebContainer environment. Environmental Variables in project settings UI inject secrets during build/runtime without hardcoding into chat history or public forks.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Client-Side Secrets
evidence.snippet: Bolt uses an .env file within its WebContainer environment. For sensitive keys, users can set Environmental Variables in the project settings UI to ensure secrets are injected during build/runtime without being hardcoded into the chat history or public forks.

dreamflow::validation_before_apply.note: Architecture-Code Sync. Validates changes against architecture.md file. AI agent ensures new code adheres to established project structure and state management rules before merging. Type-safe controls for widget properties with intelligent suggestions and validation.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Architecture-Code Sync
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow validates changes against your architecture.md file. The AI agent acts as a validator to ensure that new code snippets adhere to the established project structure and state management rules before they are merged into the main codebase. Type-safe controls for every widget property with intelligent suggestions and validation.

lovable::validation_before_apply.note: Visual & Logic Verification. Browser Testing agent runs app in virtual browser to verify changes. Pre-deployment checks ensure code doesn't break existing Supabase schemas or frontend logic.

evidence.title: Lovable Visual & Logic Verification
evidence.snippet: Lovable employs a "Browser Testing" agent that runs your app in a virtual browser to verify changes. It validates that the UI is functional and uses pre-deployment checks to ensure the code doesn't break existing Supabase schemas or frontend logic.

vibecode::validation_before_apply.note: Manual Verification. Relies on user to preview app in live canvas. Optimized for speed and 'vibing,' lacks automated unit testing or rigorous validation gates found in enterprise tools.

evidence.title: Vibecode Manual Verification
evidence.snippet: Vibecode relies primarily on the user to preview the app in the live canvas. Because it is optimized for speed and "vibing," it lacks the automated unit testing or rigorous validation gates found in enterprise-focused tools like Builder.io.

builder::validation_before_apply.note: Visual Diff & Draft Stages. Draft and Revision history system. Preview changes in staging environment. Visual Copilot compares new vs. old state to ensure no visual regressions before Publish.

evidence.title: Builder.io Visual Diff & Draft Stages
evidence.snippet: Builder provides a Draft and Revision history system. You can preview changes in a staging environment and use the Visual Copilot to compare the "new" vs. "old" state to ensure no visual regressions occurred before hitting "Publish."

bolt::validation_before_apply.note: Runtime Error Detection. Runs code in WebContainer as real-time validation gate. Terminal logs failures, AI automatically offers to fix errors before continuing. Creates commits only when changes don't break the project.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Runtime Error Detection
evidence.snippet: Bolt runs your code in a WebContainer that acts as a real-time validation gate. If the AI generates code that causes a crash or a runtime error, the terminal logs the failure, and the AI automatically offers to fix the error before you continue.

dreamflow::auto_rollback.note: Version History (Internal). Maintains history of Dreaming sessions, allows going back to previous app evolution points. Uses internal versioning rather than formal Git branching UI. No automatic failure detection rollback.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Version History
evidence.snippet: Dreamflow focuses on a persistent architecture state. While it maintains a history of your "Dreaming" sessions (allowing you to go back to previous points in the app's evolution), it does not currently expose a formal Git-branching UI where you can switch between feature/login and main within the tool itself.

lovable::auto_rollback.note: Native Git Integration. Creates commits when AI makes changes. Operates primarily on main/active branch, manual Git branching possible in connected repo. No automatic rollback on detected failures.

evidence.title: Lovable Native Git Integration
evidence.snippet: Lovable allows you to connect a GitHub repository directly. When the AI makes changes, it creates commits. While the AI agent currently operates primarily on a main/active branch, you can manually use standard Git branching in the connected repository to manage different versions of your app.

vibecode::auto_rollback.note: No Native Support. Vibe-first prototyping tool lacks internal Git management or branching. Version management requires exporting code to Cursor or IDE with Git.

evidence.title: Vibecode No Native Git Support
evidence.snippet: Vibecode is a vibe-first prototyping tool that does not have any built-in Git management or branching mechanisms. Any version management or rollback would require you to export the code to Cursor or another IDE with Git.

builder::auto_rollback.note: Full Enterprise Support. Workspaces and Content Versioning with draft versions in isolated environments before production merge. Spaces/Targeting system mimics branching. Manual rollback via version history, not automatic on failure.

evidence.title: Builder.io Workspaces and Versioning
evidence.snippet: Builder.io has full enterprise support for workspaces and content versioning, allowing teams to work on draft versions in isolated environments before merging to production. The Spaces and Targeting system can effectively mimic branching strategies. Manual rollback is available through version history.

bolt::auto_rollback.note: Manual/Export-based. Single-session WebContainer with Push to GitHub. No built-in UI for managing multiple Git branches. Users push to GitHub and create branches externally. No automatic rollback.

evidence.title: Bolt.new Manual Branching
evidence.snippet: Bolt.new operates in a single-session WebContainer environment. It allows you to push your project to GitHub. There is no built-in UI for managing multiple Git branches within Bolt.new itself. Users can push to GitHub and then manage branches externally. No automatic rollback on failures.
